blocks.content: The days after were careful. Texts came — shorter than usual, warmer than the fight, not quite back to normal.
rough cut is rough. aptly named
Eli, who somehow always knew, called Kit on a Wednesday.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Kit. I can tell something happened from the way both of you are texting the group chat. You've both gone polite. You're never both polite at the same time unless something's wrong.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We had a thing. It's fine now.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: A disagreement. We worked it out.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: About — everything. About what we are. About the distance. About the fact that neither of us has actually named it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She said she's scared to name it because if it breaks she loses me. Not just — me. Me.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: She's right to be scared of that.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Kit. At some point the fear of losing it can't be bigger than the thing itself. You understand that right.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: December. You'll both be home in December.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Figure it out then. In person. Not over a phone.
Kit looked at the ceiling of his dorm room. Darius was already asleep.
